title: Arc 1
description: Arc (Atomically Reference Counted) allows multiple threads to share ownership of data safely.
instructions: Use Arc to share data between threads.
hints: Arc::clone() creates a new reference to the same data.
Clone the Arc before the move closure: let numbers = Arc::clone(&numbers);
Each thread gets its own Arc, which increments the reference count atomically.
explanation: Arc<T> (Atomic Reference Counting) enables safe shared ownership across threads. Unlike Rc<T>, Arc uses atomic operations for its reference count, making it thread-safe. Call `Arc::clone(&arc)` to create a new handle - this is cheap (just incrementing a counter, no data copy). When the last Arc is dropped, the data is freed.
